label: Cordonmark Storehouse
description: Cordonmark Storehouse occupies a stout brick shell reinforced with salvaged ashlar, its slit windows barred and its doors banded with iron against both theft and sanctioned seizure. Interior aisles run between numbered bays of stacked crates and bale racks, with raised catwalks allowing tally-work above the floor. Civic hazard placards and interdiction stamps hang beside chained ledger boards, indicating permitted contents, quarantine intervals, and penalties under Varric Holdfast’s codified law. A lime-washed inspection strip near the threshold remains kept bare for searches, weighings, and the sealing of cargos deemed fit for circulation.
